description: Programming paradigms, language design, and computational models.

facts.truth: side effects in pure functions (IO via monads)
distractors: all forms of recursion including tail recursion
higher-order functions taking functions as arguments
pattern matching on algebraic data types

facts.truth: infinite data structures and avoids unneeded computation
distractors: faster execution than strict evaluation always
easier memory management with no thunk overhead
simpler debugging with predictable evaluation order

facts.context: Tail call optimization eliminates stack frames when:
facts.truth: recursive call is the last operation
distractors: any function calls another function at any point
the recursion depth exceeds a certain threshold
the compiler detects infinite recursion patterns

facts.context: Prototype-based OOP (JavaScript) differs from class-based by:
facts.truth: objects inheriting directly from other objects
distractors: requiring explicit class definitions for all objects
prohibiting inheritance entirely between objects
using static typing instead of dynamic typing

facts.context: The diamond problem in multiple inheritance arises when:
facts.truth: ambiguous which parent's method to inherit
distractors: a class has more than four parent classes
inheritance depth exceeds compiler limits
two unrelated classes share the same name
imperative step-by-step instruction execution
functional application and reduction only
object message passing between instances

facts.context: Homoiconicity (Lisp) means code is represented as:
facts.truth: data structures the language can manipulate
distractors: binary machine code that cannot be inspected
plain text strings without structure
separate AST format inaccessible to programs
all macro usage requiring explicit opt-in
macros from being recursive or nested
any compile-time code generation at all

facts.context: Algebraic effects (Eff, Koka) track side effects by:
distractors: prohibiting all effects like pure functional languages
runtime monitoring with no compile-time checking
automatic effect inference without any annotations
only documentation with no runtime checks ever
legal agreements between software vendors
memory layout contracts for interoperability

facts.truth: the rest of the computation as a function
distractors: only the current function's local variables
error handling context for exceptions only
loop iteration state for break/continue

facts.context: Exhaustiveness checking for pattern matching ensures:
facts.truth: all cases are handled at compile time
distractors: patterns execute in a specific runtime order
only one pattern matches each input value
patterns are optimized for fastest matching
